summary.intent: Refactor gossamer (a Claude Code session viewer) from an Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension to eliminate install friction and improve workflow integration with Claude Code sessions.
summary.outcome: Successfully built a fully functional VS Code extension with sessions list, session detail view, native checkpoint tree sidebar, embedded MCP server at /mcp, QuickPick search with FTS, terminal-spawned sessions, VS Code theme integration, and image rendering support.

repo: gossamer's architecture: Entire.io CLI writes session data to a git branch (entire/checkpoints/v1), gossamer indexes that branch into per-project SQLite DBs registered in ~/.gossamer/config.json
Each project has its own DB — never use a shared/global DB; schema migrations must target all project DBs individually
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin maps checkpointId (12-char hex) to gitOid; ShadowSession.cwd holds the session's working directory and is needed to locate the correct git repo for operations
Images are stored as base64 in full.jsonl files on the checkpoint branch but were never persisted in the LogContent schema — required a new imageData/imageMediaType column + backfill migration
The gossamer server uses repoDir (configured workspace) for all git ops; multi-repo setups require looking up the correct repo from ShadowSession.cwd or falling back to all config repos
findRepo() uses exact match; when cwd is a subdirectory of the registered repo root, findRepoForPath() with prefix matching is required (added to config.ts)
DEFAULT_PORT = 3456 is now the stable configured port; previously getFreePort() picked a random port making MCP config impossible

code.finding: process.execPath in a VS Code extension points to Electron's bundled Node.js, not the system node — always use execFileSync('which', ['node']) to get the system node for spawning processes that use native modules
Must use 'include': ['src/**/*.ts'] not a file list — listing specific files caused SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts to never compile, producing silent runtime failures
acquireVsCodeApi() can only be called once per webview; if the HTML also calls it (e.g. for a minimize button), the React module must reuse window.__vscodeApi rather than call it again

Mantine ScrollArea's viewport ref is null at the [] useEffect because the viewport renders asynchronously — check scroll position inline at the moment new content arrives rather than attaching a separate scroll listener
VS Code webview CSP needs explicit 'img-src data: https:' to render base64 images; default-src 'none' blocks all images including data: URIs silently
Vite root/outDir must be absolute paths (using resolve(__dirname, ...)) — relative paths resolve from CWD (repo root when run via npm workspace -w flag), not the package directory

After moving files to packages/core/src/, prisma generated client import path is ../prisma/generated/client/index.js (one level up), not ../../prisma/... — monorepo restructuring changes relative depths
repoName in the sessions list was using basename(repoDir) globally — should be basename(session.cwd) per-session to show the correct repository for each session

workflow: VS Code extension host runs on Electron's Node.js (different NODE_MODULE_VERSION than system node) — avoid importing native modules (better-sqlite3, Prisma) directly; spawn a child process using system node instead

When a VS Code webview tab is dragged to a new window, the webview reloads from scratch — re-send any initialization messages (like server_ready) in onDidChangeViewState when the panel becomes visible
For VS Code diffs with in-memory content, TextDocumentContentProvider + gossamer-diff: URI scheme is cleaner than temp files — no disk writes, inherently read-only, no filename collision issues
MCP stateless mode (sessionIdGenerator: undefined) with StreamableHTTPServerTransport lets you embed an MCP server directly in an existing Express app at a /mcp route
npm workspaces -w flag requires the workspace name (e.g. -w gossamer-vscode), not the directory name (e.g. -w vscode)
Git worktrees can't check out the same branch twice — create a new branch (e.g. testing from mcp) for the worktree

friction: better-sqlite3 native module NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch between system Node and VS Code's Electron Node — required significant architectural change (spawning child process) to resolve
tsconfig.extension.json include list missing new files caused silent runtime failures (blank diff views) with no build errors — very hard to diagnose
Server startup race condition: webview fetched sessions before the server finished schema push; required polling + server_ready message protocol
Prisma path misconfiguration after monorepo restructuring (../../ vs ../ prefix) — caused build failures
Vite relative path resolution for root/outDir only exposed when running via npm workspace -w flag (different CWD)
CSP blocking data: URIs for image rendering — silent failure with no console error
Random port from getFreePort() was fundamentally incompatible with static MCP config — required architectural change to stable configured port
Image data was stored in JSONL but never persisted in the DB schema — required schema migration + backfill for existing sessions
Multiple context window exhaustions requiring summary-based continuations across a very long session
open_items: Show session name/rename in the sessions table if a session has been renamed (tabled by user)
Electron desktop app was intentionally broken during refactor and never restored — it remains in the repo but non-functional
Existing sessions need manual re-sync to pick up newly indexed image data; no automatic re-indexing trigger
MCP stdio wrapper (dist/mcp.js) still exists as fallback but is now redundant given the embedded /mcp endpoint
Onboarding flow only checks for .entire/settings.json presence — no validation of Entire.io CLI version or proper configuration
testing branch/worktree at ~/gossamer-testing was created for isolated builds but may need cleanup or merging
The install-mcp.mjs script handles Claude Code and Codex CLI but no other MCP clients
fs.watch fallback polls (30s) added for reliability — could evaluate if they're still needed once file watcher stability is confirmed
